mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, defined by a 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over three months, triggers recession risk premiums that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint expansion of industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized growth across cyclical sectors, which boosts forward earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined index of INDPRO and HOUST percentiles shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or the signal flips direction during supply-chain disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show no correlation with subsequent 20-day equity returns, or the signal fails to predict returns during stagflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses a 230 threshold, inflation risk premiums spike and discount rates rise, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPI crosses the 230 threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous increase in total nonfarm payrolls and consumer credit outstanding confirms durable household spending power, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through confirmed earnings growth.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and RSAFS shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns, or the signal produces negative returns when credit growth outpaces employment growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects optimal capacity utilization and supply-side efficiency, which reduces input cost volatility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns during supply-chain bottlenecks, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding 50 units over three months acts as a leading indicator of broader demand contraction and financial stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite sharp housing permit declines, or the signal fails to predict returns when housing weakness is isolated from broader economic data.
